text: Patient reports a dull ache in the lower back that occasionally flares up when bending over. The discomfort is manageable and does not interfere with daily activities.

The patient describes an intense, throbbing headache behind the right eye that has persisted for three days. Light and noise make it significantly worse, and over-the-counter medication provides only brief relief.
After the dental procedure, the patient experienced mild tenderness at the injection site. The sensation faded within a few hours and required no additional pain management.
The patient presents with severe abdominal cramping that comes in waves every five to ten minutes. She rates the pain as the worst she has experienced since childbirth and is unable to sit still.
The runner noticed a slight twinge in his left knee during the last mile of training. There is no swelling, and the discomfort disappeared after stretching and icing the area for ten minutes.
